title: Creative Localization
description: Provider-neutral localization request for one creative. The creative's top-level `assets` are the source variant. Every target variant is explicit: omitting localization never asks a provider to translate. When this object is supplied on an existing creative it replaces that creative's complete locale set; omit the field to leave localization unchanged, or send `localization: null` to remove localization. Locale and locale_variant_id values MUST be unique within the creative, and no target locale may equal the source locale.

source.description: Identity and locale of the source/default variant. Its asset payload is the parent creative's top-level `assets` object.

locale_variant_id.description: Buyer-assigned stable identity for the source locale variant. This is a separate namespace from build_variant_id and served delivery variant_id.

target_variants.description: Complete desired target-locale set for this creative. Buyer-supplied variants carry only the locale-specific asset overrides; omitted slots inherit the source assets. Provider-generated variants carry no assets and explicitly ask the provider to translate from the source. After inheritance or generation, every resolved variant MUST satisfy the selected creative format.

locale_variant_id.description: Buyer-assigned stable identity for this target locale variant. The same value round-trips through sync_creatives and list_creatives.

translation_mode.description: `buyer_supplied` means the buyer provided explicit locale-specific asset overrides. `provider_generated` explicitly requests provider-native translation from the source variant. Merely supplying a source asset never implies provider-generated translation.

assets.description: Locale-specific asset overrides keyed by the same slot IDs as the source creative. Required for buyer_supplied; forbidden for provider_generated. Missing slots inherit the source assets.
